title: Protocol to process Gastruloids for FACS (reporters only)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"61fft","text":"Protocol to process reporter Gastruloids for FACS analysis of their endogenous reporters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"78g7i","text":"This protocol assumes standard aseptic technique, and work in a laminar flow hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ardn6","text":"Prepare enough FACS tubes/samples/controls to have a complete setup with both positive, negative, FMO (Fluorescence Minus One) references. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fq0ml","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"578q8","text":"For a simple FACS setup of Gastruloids with a double reporter, in addition to ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ftq6c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ig3p","text":"your experimental samples  (i.e. dissociated Gastruloids for each timepoint, with DAPI as live-dead discriminant)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"894b0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgll7","text":"you will thus also need ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c91o6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ag7e7","text":"double negative cells (ideally, the parental cells of the reporter, with no fluorescent insert; but use alternatively 2D cultured reporter cells if they do not express the reporters in the pluripotent state)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"25goo","text":"cells that are RFP+ and have no GFP reporter (or RFP+ beads)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ciaq","text":"cells that are GFP+ and have no RFP reporter (or GFP+ beads)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fhn7c","text":"Make sure to have a sample that not only has no reporter expression at all, but also no DAPI staining (to help set the gate for the DAPI signal).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3adin","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e20fn","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"190oo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"djha9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6207C587341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":390,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"image.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/dqqg9fk6.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/dqqe9fk6.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/dqqf9fk6.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/dqqd9fk6.webp","width":515}}}}

Roche]                                                             1:500 from  50mg/mL stock ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":11,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"duc6","text":"top up with PBS-/-","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":12,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fbgq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b4dj6","text":"Staining buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7dldk","text":"​Final concentrations: 10%ES-FBS, [100U/mL] Pen-Strep, [1mM] EDTA in PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":72}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71cuf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dckqj","text":"​1:10 final volume: ES-FBS [CAT#16141079; Gibco™/Thermo Fisher Scientific]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":20,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqos4","text":"1:500 final volume: 500mM EDTA [CAT#15575020; Gibco™/Thermo Fisher Scientific]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":26,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjr9q","text":"top up with PBS-/- [CAT#10010056; Gibco™/Thermo Fisher Scientific]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":11,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5dd4f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rkrc","text":"2% PFA in PBS-/- ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"725tn","text":"(e.g. diluted 1:2 in PBS-/- from 4%PFA solution, CAT#15434389, Alfa-Aesar/Fisher Scientific)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Once all Gastruloids have settled to the bottom of the tube, aspirate out the supernatant (N2B27 medium carried over with each Gastruloids)
Resuspend the Gastruloids in 5mL PBS-/-, to wash away traces of N2B27
Once all Gastruloids have re-settled to the bottom of the tube, aspirate out the PBS-/-. Gastruloids are ready to be digested.
Digest the Gastruloids by adding 1mL Digestion Solution (3mg/mL Collagenase IV, 4mg/mL Dispase, 100ug/mL DNAseI, in PBS-/-), 37 °C, (waterbath) , 00:04:00 min
After the 4min incubation has elapsed, use a P1000 coated in Staining Buffer to try to mechanically disrupt the Gastruloids
Place the tube back again at 37 °C, (waterbath) , 00:04:00 min; to complete the digestion

After the last 4min of incubation have elapsed, use a P1000 coated in Staining Buffer (see recipe in "Materials" section) to mechanically disrupt the Gastruloids. Gastruloids should break easily and give rise to a single cell suspension.

Prewet the blue cap of a filter-FACS tube (e.g. CAT#352235, Falcon/Corning), by pipetting 1mL of Staining Buffer through it. This step is very imprtant. Not prewetting the filter can lead to loss of a high number of cells at the filtering step.
Working On ice Filter your cell suspension (digested Gastruloids) through the cap of the FACS tube
On ice Wash the filter by passing 1mL more of DIgestion Solution through it.
On ice Transfer the contents of the FACS tube to a clean 15mL Falcon tube.
On ice Collect any leftover cells by flushing the old FACS tube with 2mL Staining Buffer, and transferring in the 15mL Falcon tube with the rest of the cells

On ice Add 5mL more Staining buffer into the tube Falcon tube with your cells suspension, to completely stop the digestion reaction. This tube now contains a filtered, single-cell suspension of your sample for FACS.
Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min
For each sample, prepare 500uL of 0.2ug/mL DAPI in Staining Buffer From a stock solution of DAPI 1mg/mL, use 1:5000. E.g. add 0.1uL DAPI in 500uL Staining Buffer
On ice Resuspend the cell pellet in the DAPI solution, and incubate 00:15:00 min, On ice
Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min
FIxation: On ice Resuspend the DAPI-stained cell pellet in 2mL 1%PFA,
Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min
Resuspend in 300uL Staining Buffer and transfer to a clean FACS tube pre-coated with Staining Buffer

For samples that come from 2D culture (e.g. 2D-grown reporter cells as negative controls, or 2D-grown single-color cells as positive controls): aspirate out the medium from the culture dish add 3mL PBS-/- to wash away any trace of medium, and aspirate it out again Add 500uL Accutase, to detach the cells, 00:05:00 min, Room temperature Transfer the detached cells +Accutase to a clean Falcon tube Add ~5mL 10% Serum to stop the reaction Spin down the cell suspension, 200 x g, 4 °C, , 00:04:00 min The cells are ready to be processed just as any Gastruloid sample (proceed to the next step)
Harvest Gastruloid from each well of a 96 well plate and collect them in a 15mL Falcon tube (pooled by timepoint or condition)
Cells can be kept in Staining Buffer, 4 °C , in the dark (wrapped in alluminium foil) for at least a week (e.g. while waiting to collect samples from later on timepoints)
When ready for FACS, On ice ,distribute your cells in 300uL per FACS tube and proceed for sorting/analysis.

If using beads as positive references, prepare bead tubes just before FACS processing. For GFP BrightComp eBeads™ Compensation Bead Kit (Invitrogen/Thermo Fisher Scientific, CAT#A10514): vortex vial for 00:00:10 s Dispense 1 drop into a clean FACS tube Resuspend in 1mL Staining Buffer
creator: Stefano D Vianello
